sections.label: Section 1.1
sections.translation: Sacred place where Alpheus breathed again ; Ortygia , scion of renowned Syracuse , bed of Artemis , sister of Delos ! From you sweet-voiced

sections.label: Section 1.5
sections.translation: song rushes out to give great praise for storm-footed horses , by the grace of Aetnaean Zeus . The chariot of Chromius and Nemea urge me to harness a song of praise for deeds of victory .

sections.label: Section 1.8
sections.translation: The foundations of the song have been laid with the gods , and with this man ’ s god-given excellence .

sections.label: Section 1.10
sections.translation: The summit of perfect glory is found in good fortune . The Muse loves to remember great contests . Sow some splendor on the island , which Zeus the lord of Olympus gave to Persephone ; he nodded assent with his flowing hair , that as the best land on the fruitful earth

sections.label: Section 1.15
sections.original: Σικελίαν πίειραν ὀρθώσειν κορυφαῖς πολίων ἀφνεαῖς .
sections.translation: he would make Sicily fertile and prosperous in her cities blossoming with wealth . And the son of Cronus sent her a people enamored of bronze-armored battle , horsemen often wedded to the golden leaves of Olympia ’ s olive . I have embarked on the occasion for many themes , without flinging a false word .

sections.label: Section 1.19
sections.translation: I have arrived singing of fine deeds at the courtyard gates

sections.label: Section 1.20
sections.translation: of a man who loves guests , where a beautifully arranged meal has been prepared for me , and the halls are often familiar with strangers from other lands . It is his lot to have noble friends to bring against his slanderers , like water against smoke .

sections.label: Section 1.25
sections.original: ἀντίον . τέχναι δʼ ἑτέρων ἕτεραι · χρὴ δʼ ἐν εὐθείαις ὁδοῖς στείχοντα μάρνασθαι φυᾷ .
sections.translation: Various men have various skills . It is right for a man to follow straight paths , and strive according to his nature .

sections.label: Section 1.26
sections.translation: For strength manifests itself in action , and intelligence in counsels , for those who have the inborn skill of foreseeing the future . Son of Hagesidamus , your way of life

sections.label: Section 1.30
sections.translation: grants you the enjoyment of many things . I take no pleasure in keeping great wealth hidden away in my hall , but in using what I have to be successful and to win a good name by helping my friends . For the hopes of men who toil much come to all alike .

sections.label: Section 2.1
sections.translation: Just as the Homeridae , the singers of woven verses , most often begin with Zeus as their prelude , so this man has received a first down-payment of victory in the sacred games by winning

sections.label: Section 2.5
sections.translation: in the grove of Nemean Zeus , which is celebrated in many hymns .

sections.label: Section 2.6
sections.translation: And if the life that guides him straight along the path of his fathers has given him as an adornment to great Athens , it must be that the son of Timonous will often reap the finest bloom of the Isthmian games , and be victorious in the Pythian contests .

sections.label: Section 2.10
sections.translation: It is right

sections.label: Section 2.11
sections.translation: for Orion to travel not far from the mountain Pleiades . And certainly Salamis can raise a warrior . In Troy Hector heard of Aias . And you , Timodemus , are exalted

sections.label: Section 2.15
sections.translation: by your enduring spirit of valor in the pancratium .

sections.label: Section 2.16
sections.translation: Acharnae has long been famous for fine men . And in everything that has to do with contests , the sons of Timodemus are proclaimed the most outstanding . Beside Parnassus , ruling on high , they carried off four victories in the games ,

sections.label: Section 2.20
sections.translation: while the men of Corinth

sections.label: Section 2.21
sections.translation: have already given them eight garlands in the glades of noble Pelops ; in the Nemean contest of Zeus they have won seven times , and at home their victories are countless . Citizens , praise Zeus in a victory procession for Timodemus ’ glorious homecoming .

sections.label: Section 2.25
sections.translation: Begin with a sweet-singing voice !

sections.label: Section 3.1
sections.original: ὦ πότνια Μοῖσα , μᾶτερ ἁμετέρα , λίσσομαι ,
sections.translation: Queenly Muse , our mother ! I entreat you , come in the sacred month of Nemea to the much-visited Dorian island of Aegina . For beside the waters of the Asopus young men are waiting , craftsmen of honey-voiced

sections.label: Section 3.5
sections.translation: victory-songs , seeking your voice . Various deeds thirst for various things ; but victory in the games loves song most of all , the most auspicious attendant of garlands and of excellence .

sections.label: Section 3.9
sections.original: τᾶς ἀφθονίαν ὄπαζε μήτιος ἀμᾶς ἄπο ·
sections.translation: Send an abundance of it , from my wisdom ;

sections.label: Section 3.10
sections.original: ἄρχε δʼ οὐρανοῦ πολυνεφέλα κρέοντι , θύγατερ ,
sections.translation: begin , divine daughter , an acceptable hymn to the ruler of the cloud-filled sky , and I will communicate it by the voices of those singers and by the lyre . The hymn will have a pleasant toil , to be the glory of the land where the ancient Myrmidons lived , whose marketplace , famous long ago ,

sections.label: Section 3.15
sections.translation: Aristocleides , through your ordinance , did not stain with dishonor by proving himself too weak in the strenuous

sections.label: Section 3.17
sections.translation: course of the pancratium . But in the deep plain of Nemea , his triumph-song brings a healing cure for wearying blows . Still , if the son of Aristophanes , who is beautiful , and whose deeds match his looks ,

sections.label: Section 3.20
sections.original: ἀνορέαις ὑπερτάταις ἐπέβα παῖς Ἀριστοφάν εο ς · οὐκέτι πρόσω
sections.translation: embarked on the highest achievements of manliness , it is not easy to cross the trackless sea beyond the pillars of Heracles ,

sections.label: Section 3.22
sections.original: ἥρως θεὸς ἃς ἔθηκε ναυτιλίας ἐσχάτας
sections.translation: which that hero and god set up as famous witnesses to the furthest limits of seafaring . He subdued the monstrous beasts in the sea , and tracked to the very end the streams of the shallows ,

sections.label: Section 3.25
sections.original: ῥοάς , ὅπα πόμπιμον κατέβαινε νόστου τέλος ,
sections.translation: where he reached the goal that sent him back home again , and he made the land known . My spirit , towards what foreign headland are you turning my voyage ? I bid you to summon the Muse in honor of Aeacus and his race ; consummate justice attends the precept , “ praise the noble .”

sections.label: Section 3.30
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἀλλοτρίων ἔρωτες ἀνδρὶ φέρειν κρέσσονες ·
sections.translation: And no man should prefer to desire what is alien . Search at home ; you have won a suitable adornment for singing something sweet . Among old examples of excellence is king Peleus , who rejoiced when he cut a matchless spear , and who alone , without an army , captured Iolcus ,

sections.label: Section 4.1
sections.translation: When toils have been resolved , festivity is the best physician ; and songs , the skillful daughters of the Muses , soothe with their touch . And warm water does not wet the limbs so gently

sections.label: Section 4.5
sections.translation: as praise that accompanies the lyre . Speech lives longer than deeds ; whatever words the tongue , with the favor of the Graces , draws from the deep mind .

sections.label: Section 4.9
sections.original: τό μοι θέμεν Κρονίδᾳ τε Δὶ καὶ Νεμέᾳ
sections.translation: May it be mine to set forth such speech , in honor of Zeus the son of Cronus , and Nemea ,

sections.label: Section 4.10
sections.translation: and Timasarchus ’ wrestling , as a prelude to my song . And may it be welcomed by the home of the Aeacids , with its fine towers , that light which shines for all , with justice that defends the stranger . And if your father Timocritus had still been warmed by the strength of the sun , playing embroidered notes on the cithara

sections.label: Section 4.15
sections.translation: and bending to this strain , he would have often celebrated his triumphant son ,

sections.label: Section 4.17
sections.original: Κλεωναίου τʼ ἀπʼ ἀγῶνος ὅρμον στεφάνων
sections.translation: because he had sent back from the contest at Cleonae a chain of garlands , and from splendid , illustrious Athens ; and because in seven-gated Thebes ,

sections.label: Section 4.20
sections.translation: beside Amphitryon ’ s splendid tomb , the Cadmeans gladly crowned him with flowers , for the sake of Aegina . For he looked on [*] a hospitable city , when he came as a friend to friends , to the prosperous court of Heracles ,

sections.label: Section 4.25
sections.original: 40 σὺν ᾧ ποτε Τρωΐαν κραταιὸς Τελαμὼν
sections.translation: with whom once powerful Telamon destroyed Troy and the Meropes and the great and terrible warrior Alcyoneus , but not before that giant had laid low , by hurling a rock , twelve chariots and twice twelve horse-taming heroes who were riding in them .

sections.label: Section 4.30
sections.original: 50 δὶς τόσους . ἀπειρομάχας ἐών κε φανείη
sections.translation: A man who did not understand this proverb would appear to be inexperienced in battle : since “ it is likely that the doer will also suffer .”

sections.label: Section 5.1
sections.original: οὐκ ἀνδριαντοποιός εἰμʼ , ὥστʼ ἐλινύσοντα ἐργάζεσθαι ἀγάλματʼ ἐπʼ αὐτᾶς βαθμίδος
sections.translation: I am not a sculptor , to make statues that stand motionless on the same pedestal . Sweet song , go on every merchant-ship and rowboat that leaves Aegina , and announce that Lampon ’ s powerful son Pytheas

sections.label: Section 5.5
sections.translation: won the victory garland for the pancratium at the Nemean games , a boy whose cheeks do not yet show the tender season that is mother to the dark blossom .

sections.label: Section 5.7
sections.original: ἐκ δὲ Κρόνου καὶ Ζηνὸς ἥρωας αἰχματὰς φυτευθέντας καὶ ἀπὸ χρυσεᾶν Νηρηΐδων
sections.translation: He has brought honor to the Aeacids , the heroic spearmen descended from Cronus and Zeus and the golden Nereids , and to his mother city , a land friendly to guests .

sections.label: Section 5.10
sections.translation: Once by the altar of father Zeus Hellenius the illustrious sons of Endais and the strong , mighty Phocus stood and prayed , stretching their hands to the sky , that the city would one day be famous for men and ships .

sections.label: Section 5.13
sections.original: ὁ τᾶς θεοῦ , ὃν Ψαμάθεια τίκτʼ ἐπὶ ῥηγμῖνι πόντου .
sections.translation: Phocus was the son of the goddess Psamatheia ; he was born by the shore of the sea . Reverence restrains me from speaking of an enormous and unjust venture ,

sections.label: Section 5.15
sections.original: πῶς δὴ λίπον εὐκλέα νᾶσον , καὶ τίς ἄνδρας ἀλκίμους
sections.translation: how indeed they left the glorious island , and what divine power drove the brave men from Oenone . I will stop : it is not always beneficial for the precise truth to show her face , and silence is often the wisest thing for a man to heed .

sections.label: Section 5.19
sections.original: εἰ δʼ ὄλβον ἢ χειρῶν βίαν ἢ σιδαρίταν ἐπαινῆσαι πόλεμον δεδόκηται , μακρά μοι
sections.translation: But if it is resolved to praise wealth , or the strength of hands , or iron war ,

sections.label: Section 5.20
sections.original: αὐτόθεν ἅλμαθʼ ὑποσκάπτοι τις · ἔχω γονάτων ἐλαφρὸν ὁρμάν ·
sections.translation: let someone mark off a long jump for me from this point . I have a light spring in my knees , and eagles swoop over the sea . The most beautiful chorus of Muses sang gladly for the Aeacids on Mt . Pelion , and among them Apollo , sweeping the seven-tongued lyre with a golden plectrum ,

sections.label: Section 5.25
sections.original: ἁγεῖτο παντοίων νόμων · αἱ δὲ πρώτιστον μὲν ὕμνησαν Διὸς ἀρχόμεναι σεμνὰν Θέτιν
sections.translation: led all types of strains . And the Muses began with a prelude to Zeus , then sang first of divine Thetis and of Peleus ; how Hippolyte , the opulent daughter of Cretheus , wanted to trap him with deceit . With elaborate planning she persuaded her husband , the watcher of the Magnesians , to be a partner in her plot , and she forged a false story ;

sections.label: Section 5.30
sections.original: ὡς ἆρα νυμφείας ἐπείρα κεῖνος ἐν λέκτροις Ἀκάστου
sections.translation: that Peleus had made an attempt on her

sections.label: Section 6.1
sections.original: ἓν ἀνδρῶν , ἓν θεῶν γένος · ἐκ μιᾶς δὲ πνέομεν
sections.translation: There is one race of men , one race of gods ; and from a single mother we both draw our breath . But all allotted power divides us : man is nothing , but for the gods the bronze sky endures as a secure home forever . Nevertheless , we bear some resemblance to the immortals , either in greatness

sections.label: Section 6.5
sections.translation: of mind or in nature , although we do not know , by day or by night , towards what goal fortune has written that we should run .

sections.label: Section 6.8
sections.original: τεκμαίρει καί νυν Ἀλκιμίδας τὸ συγγενὲς ἰδεῖν
sections.translation: Even now Alcimidas gives visible proof that his hereditary qualities are like the fruitful fields , which , in alternation ,

sections.label: Section 6.10
sections.original: τόκα μὲν ὦν βίον ἀνδράσιν ἐπηετανὸν πεδίων ἔδοσαν ,
sections.translation: at one time give men yearly sustenance from the plains , and at another time gather strength from repose . He has come from the lovely games of Nemea , the athletic boy who , pursuing this ordinance of Zeus , has shown that he is a successful hunter in the wrestling ring ,

sections.label: Section 6.15
sections.original: ἴχνεσιν ἐν Πραξιδάμαντος ἑὸν πόδα νέμων
sections.translation: by planting his step in the tracks of his grandfather , his blood-relative . For that man , an Olympic victor , was the first to bring garlands from the Alpheus to the Aeacidae ; and he had himself crowned five times at the Isthmus ,

sections.label: Section 6.20
sections.translation: and three times at Nemea , putting an end to the obscurity of Socleides , who proved to be the greatest of the sons of Hagesimachus ,

sections.label: Section 6.24
sections.original: ἐπεί οἱ τρεῖς ἀεθλοφόροι πρὸς ἄκρον ἀρετᾶς
sections.translation: since he had three victorious sons who reached the summit of excellence ,

sections.label: Section 6.25
sections.original: 40 ἦλθον , οἵτε πόνων ἐγεύσαντο . σὺν θεοῦ δὲ τύχᾳ
sections.translation: and who had a taste of toils . With the favorable fortune of the gods , no other family has been proclaimed by the boxing contest in the center of all Greece as the guardian of more garlands . I hope , with this great praise , to hit the target squarely , like one who shoots from a bow . Come , Muse , give a straight course to the glorious wind of song for this man .

sections.label: Section 6.30
sections.translation: For when men pass away

sections.label: Section 7.1
sections.translation: [*] Eleithuia , seated beside the deep-thinking Fates , hear me , creator of offspring , child of Hera great in strength . Without you we see neither the light nor the dark night before it is our lot to go to your sister , Hebe , [*] with her lovely limbs .

sections.label: Section 7.5
sections.original: ἀναπνέομεν δʼ οὐχ ἅπαντες ἐπὶ ἴσα ·
sections.translation: Yet we do not all draw our first breath for equal ends . Under the yoke of destiny , different men are held by different restraints . But it is by your favor that , even so , Sogenes the son of Thearion , distinguished by his excellence , is celebrated in song as glorious among pentathletes .
